Discord GitHub
Get Started

Tool reference

Every Tool, Explained

8 MCP tools + a full CLI working together to minimize tokens while preserving the context your AI needs for high-quality reasoning.

89-99% savings

ctx_read

Smart File Read with 6 Modes

The core tool. Session-aware caching means files are only transmitted once. Re-reads return a 13-token cache hit. Six distinct modes let you choose exactly how much context the LLM receives — from dependency maps to full content.

Modes

full ~99% on re-reads

Default. Reads the file with structured headers (deps, exports) and caches it. Re-reads return a compact cache-hit message.

map 85-95%

Dependency graph + exports + API signatures. Use for context files you won't edit. Structured headers show imports/exports at a glance.

signatures 83-96%

AST-powered extraction of function signatures, interfaces, types, and exports via tree-sitter. Supports 10 languages: TS/JS, Rust, Python, Go, Java, C, C++, Ruby.

diff 70-95%

Returns only lines that changed since the last read. Perfect for monitoring edits.

aggressive 20-40%

Full content with syntax stripping: reduced indentation, removed comments, stripped empty lines.

entropy 60-85%

Shannon entropy filtering removes low-information lines. Jaccard deduplication collapses similar blocks.

ctx_read

$ ctx_read auth.ts --mode map

F1=auth.ts [123L]
  deps: db, crypto, session
  exports: createUser, authenticateUser, validateSession
  API:
    fn async createUser(email:s, password:s) → User
    fn async authenticateUser(email:s, password:s) → Session
    fn async validateSession(token:s) → boolean
[794 tok saved (93%)]
40% savings

ctx_tree

Compact Project Structure

Token-efficient directory listings using indentation instead of Unicode box-drawing. Shows file counts per directory so the LLM understands project scale without reading every path.

ctx_tree

$ ctx_tree --depth 2

src/ (45)
  core/ (8)
  tools/ (5)
  dashboard/ (2)
  patterns/ (5)
package.json
README.md
[64 tok saved (34%)]
60-90% savings

ctx_shell

CLI Output Compression

50+ compression patterns across 12 categories — git, npm, cargo, docker, kubectl, gh, pip, ruff, eslint, prettier, tsc, curl, wget, and test runners. Also available as a transparent shell hook that compresses output automatically without LLM cooperation.

ctx_shell

$ ctx_shell "git status"

feature/auth ↑3
staged: +session.ts ~login.ts ~api.ts
unstaged: ~session.ts
untracked: session.test.ts
[534 tok saved (87%)]
90-95% savings

ctx_compress

Context Checkpoint

Compresses all cached files into an ultra-compact signature map. Use as a memory checkpoint when conversations get long — paste the output into a fresh chat to carry over full project understanding at minimal token cost.

ctx_compress

$ ctx_compress

CTX CHECKPOINT (12 files)

F1 auth.ts [123L]: fn generateToken, fn createUser, ...
F2 db.ts [89L]: cl Database, fn query, fn migrate
F3 routes.ts [234L]: fn setupRoutes, fn authMiddleware

STATS: 34 reads, 22 hits (65%)
COMPRESSION: 28450 -> 890 tok (97% saved)
Data-driven savings

ctx_benchmark

Exact Token Measurements

Benchmarks any file against all compression strategies using tiktoken. Shows exact token counts side-by-side so you can make data-driven decisions about which mode to use.

ctx_benchmark

$ ctx_benchmark auth.ts

Benchmark: auth.ts (123L)

Strategy                     Tokens   Savings
──────────────────────────────────────────────
raw                             915  —
aggressive                      860  -55 (6%)
signatures (compact)            121  -794 (87%)
signatures (tdd)                 98  -817 (89%)
entropy                         198  -717 (78%)
full + §MAP (tdd)               880  -35 (4%)
aggressive + §MAP (tdd)         825  -90 (10%)
cache hit                        13  -902 (99%)
──────────────────────────────────────────────
Best: "cache hit" saves 902 tokens (99%)
TDD bonus (signatures): 23 extra tokens saved (19%)
Monitoring savings

ctx_metrics

Real-time Session Statistics

Session-wide statistics with tiktoken-measured token counts and USD cost estimates. Tracks cache hit rates, per-tool and per-mode savings, and projected session costs.

ctx_metrics

$ ctx_metrics

§metrics
════════════════════════════════════════
files:12 reads:34 hits:22 (65%)
tok: 28.5K→4.1K | saved:24.3K (85.5%)
cost: $0.4270→$0.0620 | -$0.3650

tool            n    orig   saved    %
────────────────────────────────────────
ctx_read       18   22.1K   19.2K  87%
ctx_shell       8    4.2K    3.1K  74%
ctx_search      5    1.8K    1.4K  78%

§refs:
  F1=auth.ts [123L 915t r:4]
  F2=db.ts [89L 678t r:3]

∴ session savings (incl. thinking): $0.912
Diagnostic savings

ctx_analyze

Information-Theoretic Analysis

Deep analysis of a file using Shannon entropy and Jaccard similarity. Shows exactly which lines carry real information vs. boilerplate, and recommends the optimal compression mode.

ctx_analyze

$ ctx_analyze middleware.ts

ANALYSIS: middleware.ts (78L, 1856 tok)

Entropy Distribution:
  H̄ = 3.2 bits/char (σ = 1.1)
  Low-entropy (H<2.0): 14 lines (18%)
  High-entropy (H>4.0): 22 lines (28%)

Pattern Analysis:
  3 similar blocks (Jaccard ≥ 0.7)
  Dedup potential: ~240 tokens

Strategy Comparison:
  raw           1,856 tok  —
  signatures      312 tok  -83%
  entropy         298 tok  -84%  ← recommended
  cache hit        13 tok  -99%

Recommendation: entropy (best first-read savings)
50-80% savings

ctx_search

Token-efficient Code Search

Searches files for patterns and returns only matching lines with compact context. Avoids the need to read entire files when looking for specific code.

ctx_search

$ ctx_search "createUser" --ext ts

3 matches in 2 files:
auth.ts:18 fn createUser(email:s, password:s, ...)
routes.ts:45 await createUser(body.email, body.pwd, ...)
routes.ts:89 // createUser validation
[~1200 tok saved vs full reads]
Auto-setup savings

lean-ctx init

One-Command Setup

Initializes lean-ctx in the current project or globally. With --global, auto-installs 23 shell aliases for git, npm, cargo, docker, kubectl, gh, pip, ruff, and more into your shell profile (.zshrc / .bashrc).

lean-ctx init

$ lean-ctx init --global

Installing lean-ctx shell aliases...
Added to ~/.zshrc:
  alias git='lean-ctx -c git'
  alias npm='lean-ctx -c npm'
  alias cargo='lean-ctx -c cargo'
  alias docker='lean-ctx -c docker'
  alias kubectl='lean-ctx -c kubectl'
  alias gh='lean-ctx -c gh'
  alias pip='lean-ctx -c pip'
  ... (23 aliases total)
Done! Restart your shell or run: source ~/.zshrc
Lifetime stats savings

lean-ctx gain

Persistent Savings Dashboard

Shows cumulative token savings across all sessions. Stats are persisted to ~/.lean-ctx/stats.json. See total tokens saved, total cost avoided, and per-tool breakdowns over your entire usage history.

lean-ctx gain

$ lean-ctx gain

lean-ctx Token Savings
══════════════════════════════════════════════════
Total commands:  47
Input tokens:    12.4K
Output tokens:   4.8K
Tokens saved:    7.6K (61.3%)
Tracking since:  2026-03-23

By Command:
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
Command               Count      Saved   Avg%
ctx_read                 18      5.2K  91.0%
ls                       12      2.1K  74.1%
ctx_shell                 8      1.4K  74.0%
git status                8        680  39.9%
LeanCTX

MIT · Rust · Open Source

Hybrid Context Optimizer.
89–99% fewer tokens for AI coding tools.
